compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
CALIBRATION-3 FLOOR-ATTRIBUTION EVIDENCE (design input for this story, per task 3dc0/relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly AC4; method pre-registered there: recompute impact_plan from each persisted verification.severity_attributes with the hard-override floor disabled; a block is floor-driven iff the floorless amplified impact falls below the finding's block_threshold while the floored value met it; thresholds from the persisted per-finding block_threshold, else current routing).

Corpus: plan-v2 segment, 1016 sidecars / 264 tickets / 2271 decision==block findings (every one carried severity_attributes).
- FLOOR-DRIVEN BLOCKS: 807 of 2271 = 35.5% of all blocking findings would NOT have blocked without the 0.85 hard override.
- ac_unverifiable-bearing blocks: 804; of these 395 are floor-driven. ac_unverifiable accounts for 48.9% of all floor-driven blocks (395/807) - the largest single override axis.
- GRADE DISTRIBUTION of the classified sample: the override fires at ANY non-none grade; in the seed-42 sample of 100 floor-driven ac_unverifiable findings, 52 fired at grade=low, 48 at grade=medium. The floor is currently grade-blind: 'low' buys the same 0.85 as 'high'.

ORACLE-KIND CLASSIFICATION (seed-42 random sample of 100 floor-driven ac_unverifiable findings; classified per the pre-registered protocol):
- underspecified_oracle: 56/100 - a check exists or is clearly constructible; the finding demands the exact command / file / expected value / definition (e.g. 'no concrete proving command named', 'default value never stated', ''render'' undefined').
- missing_oracle: 7/100 - the finding asserts no verification method exists at all (deliverable with no covering AC; 'verified only by human inspection'; non-durable external action).
- ambiguous: 37/100 - dominated by a THIRD failure mode the split design must decide on: the BROKEN oracle (a proving command/symbol/count that is factually wrong - nonexistent test paths, wrong CLI entry point, asserts 'exactly 4' where 5 exist, contradictory ACs). A broken oracle is arguably block-worthy (the stated verification CANNOT pass), unlike an underspecified one.

DESIGN IMPLICATIONS for the split: (1) the dominant floor-driven ac_unverifiable population (56%) is exactly the 'valid-but-terminal-detail' block class this story exists to demote - grading it below the blocking tier removes up to ~14% of ALL plan-v2 blocks (395 * 0.56 / 2271 ~ 9.7% conservatively from this axis alone, more if the other three override axes show the same pattern). (2) Consider a THREE-grade axis (missing / broken / underspecified) rather than two, or fold 'broken' into the floor-retaining grade: 37% of the sample is broken-oracle-shaped and demoting those would weaken a genuinely protective check. (3) The verify-pass rubric must define the grades with examples of each; the current binary fires floor-blind at grade=low in a majority of sampled cases.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The plan-v2 impact model floors four failure-mode axes at 0.85 (above every blocking threshold) whenever the axis is non-none at ANY grade - the floor is grade-blind. Calibration 3 (task relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly; evidence comment on this story, 2026-07-15) measured the consequence on the plan-v2 segment: 35.5% of ALL blocking findings (807/2271) are floor-driven (would not block without the override), ac_unverifiable alone is 48.9% of the floor-driven set, 52/100 of a classified sample fired at grade=low, and the oracle-kind classification split 56% underspecified_oracle / 37% broken-oracle-shaped / 7% missing_oracle. The dominant floor-driven class is "a check exists, the plan just doesn't spell out the exact command/file/value" - a refinement demand that today auto-blocks with the same force as a genuinely untestable criterion. This story splits the axis so severity follows the actual defect.

## What
Replace the binary-ish ac_unverifiable severity grading with a THREE-value oracle-kind grade in the plan-review verification model, assigned explicitly by the pass-2 verifier (the calibration-3 evidence motivates three, not two - a broken oracle is not a refinement):
- missing_oracle: no verification method exists or could exist as the criterion is phrased (7% of sample) - KEEPS the 0.85 hard floor.
- broken_oracle: a stated proving command/symbol/count is factually wrong - nonexistent path, wrong CLI, contradictory assertion - so the stated verification CANNOT pass (37% of sample) - KEEPS the 0.85 hard floor (the check is protective; these are real plan defects).
- underspecified_oracle: a check exists or is clearly constructible; the finding demands the exact command/file/expected value (56% of sample) - scores BELOW the lowest blocking threshold in force after calibration 3 (0.60), i.e. maps to an impact contribution <= 0.55, so it surfaces and is coached but never auto-blocks.
BOTH verifier prompts define the three grades with one worked example each drawn from the calibration-3 classified sample (the gate YAML routes to TWO prompt files: src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier.md AND plan_review_verifier_agentic.md - both change in lockstep). SCORING MECHANICS (exact decide.py change): impact_plan currently scores all seven axes through the shared ordinal map _SEV01 ({none,low,medium,high}) in TWO generic loops - the contribs list comprehension over _PLAN_SEVERITY_AXES and the has_override any() over _PLAN_HARD_OVERRIDE_AXES. ac_unverifiable is REMOVED from both generic loops and special-cased: (1) contribs = [_SEV01.get(attrs.get(a), 0.0) for a in _PLAN_SEVERITY_AXES if a != "ac_unverifiable"] + [ORACLE_GRADE01.get(attrs.get("ac_unverifiable"), 0.0)]; (2) has_override = any(_SEV01.get(attrs.get(a), 0.0) > 0.0 for a in _PLAN_HARD_OVERRIDE_AXES if a != "ac_unverifiable") or attrs.get("ac_unverifiable") in ("broken_oracle", "missing_oracle"). ORACLE_GRADE01 = {"none": 0.0, "underspecified_oracle": UNDERSPECIFIED_ORACLE_CONTRIB, "broken_oracle": 1.0, "missing_oracle": 1.0} with UNDERSPECIFIED_ORACLE_CONTRIB = 0.55 defined as a named module constant whose comment states its invariant (strictly below the lowest blocking threshold in criteria_routing.json), and a unit test asserts UNDERSPECIFIED_ORACLE_CONTRIB < min(block_threshold over blocking-posture criteria) so a future threshold recalibration below 0.55 fails loudly instead of silently re-enabling blocks. An unknown/legacy grade string maps to 0.0 via .get() default in plan-v3 scoring - legacy records are never scored with plan-v3 code anyway (segmentation, below). The other six axes keep _SEV01 and the any-non-none override unchanged. WRITE-TIME ENFORCEMENT (the concrete mechanism): src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py owns PlanSeverityAttrs; its ac_unverifiable field changes from the ordinal type to Literal["none", "underspecified_oracle", "broken_oracle", "missing_oracle"], so Pydantic strict validation rejects out-of-vocabulary grades at verification-parse time - that field is the closed-set gate the ACs pin. OPERATOR-ATTESTED ENRICH: workflow_ops.enrich_operator_attested's guard changes from `not in (None, "", "none")` to the explicit clearable set `in ("missing_oracle", "underspecified_oracle")` (clears to "none" when attested evidence satisfies the criterion - the recorded attestation IS the oracle) while broken_oracle is never cleared (a factually wrong stated command is not cured by an attestation) - this file is in Scope and the asymmetry is pinned by test. BACK-COMPAT: the closed-set validation applies to NEW plan-v3 verifications at parse time; already-stored plan-v2 sidecars keep their legacy {low,medium,high} grades and are read as-is - every calibration/replay consumer segments by impact_model_version (ADR 0036), so old records are never scored with new semantics - and this is pinned by a TEST (a plan-v2 fixture sidecar with grade "low" round-trips unmodified through latest_review_result/all_review_results). The sidecar SCHEMA id stays plan_review_result_v2: the payload shape (keys, nesting) is unchanged - only the value vocabulary of one severity-attribute field changes - so the schema-string guard in the readers keeps matching; IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION (plan-v3) is the segmentation key for the semantics change. ROLLBACK: the version bump makes back-out clean - reverting the change restores plan-v2 semantics and the corpora stay segmented; no expand-contract migration is needed because sidecars are immutable records, not migrated state. IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bumps plan-v2 -> plan-v3 in the same change. The other three override axes (dod_uncertifiable, undecomposed, divergent_implementation) keep their ordinal vocabulary and any-non-none floor, untouched in this story (their grade mix is unmeasured; calibration 4 scope).

## Scope
- src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py: ORACLE_GRADE01 + UNDERSPECIFIED_ORACLE_CONTRIB constants; ac_unverifiable excluded from both generic _SEV01 loops and special-cased per the exact change spec above.
- src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py: PlanSeverityAttrs.ac_unverifiable becomes the closed Literal grade type (the write-time enforcement mechanism).
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py: enrich_operator_attested vocabulary update (clears missing/underspecified on attestation, never broken_oracle).
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier.md AND plan_review_verifier_agentic.md: three-grade definitions + one worked example each; the plan-review verification output schema accepts exactly {none, underspecified_oracle, broken_oracle, missing_oracle} for the axis and rejects anything else at write time.
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump to plan-v3; the grade persists in verification.severity_attributes as today (values change, key stays).
- Unit tests: tests/unit/test_impact_plan.py (replace old low/medium/high ac_unverifiable pins with the three-grade pins; keep other-axis pins), tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py (plan-v2 -> plan-v3 assertion), tests/unit/_audit_page_helpers.py (hardcoded plan-v2 string updated), new pins for: schema acceptance AND rejection of an invalid grade, sidecar persistence, the enrich asymmetry, the UNDERSPECIFIED_ORACLE_CONTRIB < min(blocking thresholds) invariant, and the plan-v2 legacy-fixture read-as-is round-trip.
- docs/plan-review-gate.md: the floor description updated to the graded rule with the back-compat note.
- Coordination: story ea39 (R5 kernel sub-answer, epic pastoral-aquatic-viper) also plans a version bump and depends_on this story - if both land close together the bumps compose (v3 then v4), never merge into one ambiguous version.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The verification model accepts exactly the closed grade set {none, underspecified_oracle, broken_oracle, missing_oracle} for ac_unverifiable on new plan-v3 verifications - pinned by BOTH an acceptance test and a rejection test for an out-of-vocabulary grade - and both verifier prompts (single-turn + agentic) define each grade with one worked example.
- [ ] Impact scoring pinned by unit tests: missing_oracle and broken_oracle findings floor at >= 0.85; underspecified_oracle findings score <= 0.55 (below the 0.60 lowest blocking threshold) and never trigger the hard floor; the other three override axes floor unchanged.
- [ ] IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION is plan-v3 in the same change (test_impact_model_versioning.py updated), the grade persists per finding in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (pinned by test), legacy plan-v2 records are read as-is (segmentation by version is the back-compat mechanism, asserted in the doc note), and enrich_operator_attested clears missing/underspecified but never broken_oracle (pinned by test).
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md documents the three-grade rule citing the calibration-3 evidence comment on this story, and the change is MERGED through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).
compiled_state.title: Split ac_unverifiable hard-floor axis into oracle-kind grades: missing/broken keep the floor, underspecified demotes (plan-v3)
